mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. It provides a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool, which is necessary for managing your Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure you have Python 3.10-3.13 installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys Langflow to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Deploy Langflow in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This enables autoscaling for Langflow.
> This sets up monitoring for Langflow.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Langflow deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the Langflow release.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Langflow to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n langflow

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades Langflow to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the Kubernetes API server is unreachable or overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check your network connection and ensure the Kubernetes cluster is running properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1' to reset your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically means there is an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resource limits for the deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n langflow' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
